2604.12493.title: Latent Planning Emerges with Scale
2604.12493.paper_summary: LLMs can perform seemingly planning-intensive tasks, like writing coherent stories or functioning code, without explicitly verbalizing a plan; however, the extent to which they implicitly plan is unknown. In this paper, we define latent planning as occurring when LLMs possess internal planning representations that (1) cause the generation of a specific future token or concept, and (2) shape preceding context to license said future token or concept. We study the Qwen-3 family (0.6B-14B) on simple planning tasks, finding that latent planning ability increases with scale. Models that plan possess features that represent a planned-for word like "accountant", and cause them to output "an" rather than "a"; moreover, even the less-successful Qwen-3 4B-8B have nascent planning mechanisms. On the more complex task of completing rhyming couplets, we find that models often identify a rhyme ahead of time, but even large models seldom plan far ahead. However, we can elicit some planning that increases with scale when steering models towards planned words in prose. In sum, we offer a framework for measuring planning and mechanistic evidence of how models' planning abilities grow with scale.
2604.12493.paper_summary_zh: LLMs 可以執行看似需要計畫的任務，例如撰寫連貫的故事或運行代碼，而不需要明確地口頭表達計畫；然而，它們在多大程度上進行隱性計畫仍然未知。<lb/>在本文中，我們將潛在計畫定義為當 LLMs 擁有內部計畫表徵時發生的情況，這些表徵 (1) 促使生成特定的未來標記或概念，並且 (2) 形塑前文上下文以授權該未來標記或概念。<lb/>我們研究了 Qwen-3 系列 (0.6B-14B) 在簡單計畫任務上的表現，發現潛在計畫能力隨著規模的增大而增加。<lb/>具有計畫的模型擁有代表計畫中的單詞（如 "accountant"）的特徵，並使它們輸出 "an" 而不是 "a"；此外，即使是較不成功的 Qwen-3 4B-8B 也具有初步的計畫機制。<lb/>在完成押韻對句的更複雜任務中，我們發現模型經常提前識別押韻，但即使是大型模型也很少能夠提前計畫。<lb/>然而，當引導模型朝向散文中的計畫單詞時，我們可以引發一些隨著規模增大而增加的計畫。<lb/>總之，我們提供了一個測量計畫的框架，以及模型計畫能力隨著規模增長的機械證據。
2604.12493.author: Michael Hanna et.al.
